positive_description: Subtle jaw and lower face reshaping. The chin becomes slightly more prominent and squared, with the jaw angle becoming marginally more defined. The lower face gains a small amount of vertical depth. The overall effect reads as a slightly stronger, more angular lower face.
negative_description: The chin recedes slightly and the jaw angle softens. The lower face becomes marginally rounder and less angular. The jaw line loses some definition, reading as softer and less prominent.
symmetry_notes: Symmetric. Left and right 3/4 views are consistent. No meaningful lateral asymmetry.
artifact_notes: Subtle component. No artifacts visible. Clean geometry throughout. The visual differences are modest — most visible in the chin and jaw angle region when comparing extremes side by side.
primary_face_region: lower face — chin prominence, jaw angle, mandibular contour
useful_for: identity noise — safe at any weight in range

interactions.beta3: beta3 controls jaw width more dramatically; beta12 fine-tunes jaw angularity and chin shape
interactions.beta2: beta2 is the primary chin projection axis; beta12 adds supplementary chin/jaw angle detail

prose: Beta12 is a subtle lower-face detail component that adjusts chin prominence and jaw angularity. Where beta3 dramatically widens or narrows the jaw, beta12 operates at a finer scale, tweaking the angle of the mandible and the forward projection of the chin. Positive values produce a slightly more angular, squared-off jaw with a more prominent chin; negative values soften and round the lower face. The effect is modest even at +/-3.0, making this a safe component for adding individual character to faces. It is bilaterally symmetric and artifact-free. Not currently used in any recipe, but its focus on jaw angularity makes it a potential complement to the dominance axis if more jaw detail variation is desired.
